title: Take metro or bus twice a week
body: Replacing 2 car commutes (10 km each) with metro saves about 3 kg CO₂ per week — like not burning 1.3 litres of petrol.

title: Switch off standby power
body: TV, Wi-Fi router, and chargers on standby can use 50–100 kWh per year. Switch off at the plug to save money and about 35–70 kg CO₂ annually.

title: Batch your Blinkit / Zepto orders
body: One combined grocery run instead of 4 small orders saves ~0.24 kg delivery CO₂ and roughly 60g of delivery bags.

title: Carry a foldable bag
body: Indians use millions of thin plastic bags daily. One cloth bag used 100 times avoids 1–2 kg of plastic waste per year.

title: Leave no trace
body: Take all wrappers and bottles home. Beach litter often burns or reaches the sea — both release carbon and harm wildlife.

title: Tiffin over delivery
body: One Swiggy/Zomato school-day lunch adds ~0.12 kg CO₂ and 50g+ packaging. Homemade lunch in a steel box is healthier and greener.

title: Use rooftop solar if you can
body: Every 100 kWh of solar in India avoids about 71 kg CO₂ from the grid (CEA factor). Even a small system makes a big difference.

title: One bottle, refill all day
body: Refilling a 1L bottle 5 times a week avoids 5 PET bottles — about 125g plastic and 0.75 kg CO₂ from production.
